question_text: What happens to particles when a substance melts?

options.label: A
options.text: Particles move closer together and stop moving

options.label: B
options.text: Particles gain kinetic energy and break free from fixed positions

options.label: D
options.text: Particles lose all their kinetic energy

explanation: During melting, heat energy is absorbed by the solid, giving particles more kinetic energy so they can break free from their fixed positions and flow as a liquid.
